title: frangula macrocarpa
intro: Frangula macrocarpa, also known as the black alder or alder buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its dark brown bark and compound leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthraquinone glycosides, which have laxative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Frangula macrocarpa include treating constipation, diarrhea, and digestive issues, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation. Frangula macrocarpa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, often used in herbal medicine to promote gastrointestinal health and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders.
